name: Brent crude oil price
evidence_text: The price for Brent crude – the international standard – went from a high of $112 a barrel overnight to below $107 in the morning before turning back higher, and after settling at $112.10.

name: Uranium stockpile
evidence_text: Iran is believed to have about 440kg (970lb) of uranium enriched to 60 percent.

name: Energy Bill Cap
evidence_text: A typical gas and electricity bill is forecast to reach £1,850 a year from July.

name: Previous Energy Bill Cap
evidence_text: the £1,641 cap on energy bills set for April to June

name: Increase in Energy Bill
evidence_text: adding £209 to a typical annual bill

name: Cost of war with Iran
evidence_text: The war has cost the country at least $29bn so far, according to Pentagon officials.

name: UK overseas aid percentage
evidence_text: The UK cut aid from 0.5% of gross national income under the last government – itself a cut from 0.7% under the previous Labour government – to 0.3%.

name: Climate finance cut
evidence_text: Climate finance, which helps developing countries protect their ecosystems and become more resilient to the impacts of extreme weather, has been cut to £2bn a year over the next three years.

name: Investment for climate in emerging markets
evidence_text: British International Investment will announce £4.6bn for climate investment in emerging markets.

name: unemployment rate
evidence_text: The unemployment rate was up in the three months to March, from 4.9% in February.

name: payrolled employees
evidence_text: The number of payrolled employees dropped sharply in April, falling by 100,000.

name: youth unemployment rate
evidence_text: The unemployment rate for 18- to 24-year-olds rose to 14.7% in the three months to March.

name: GDP growth
evidence_text: Figures from the ONS released last week showed the UK economy grew by 0.3% in March.

name: Profit after tax
evidence_text: Ryanair reported a record profit after tax of €2.26bn (£2bn) in its financial year ended in March.

name: Environmental taxes increase
evidence_text: The company also flagged that it expected its environmental taxes in the EU to increase by €300m this year to about €1.4bn.

name: Oil Price
evidence_text: Minutes before the president's announcement, petroleum futures had been trading at $108.83 a barrel. His word about negotiations almost instantly shaved more than $2 off the price, but it crept up again and ended on Monday at $107.25 a barrel.

name: Number of homes demolished
evidence_text: 48 homes have been demolished since 2023, according to B'Tselem.

name: Number of people facing expulsion
evidence_text: 1,450 people there now face expulsion.

name: Unemployment Rate
evidence_text: The UK unemployment rate jumped back to 5% in March.

name: Payrolled Jobs Decline
evidence_text: The number of payrolled jobs in the economy fell 100,000.

name: Regular Pay Growth
evidence_text: Regular pay, excluding bonuses, increased at a rate of just 3.4% from January to March.

name: Payment to FBI agent
evidence_text: Al-Saadi is alleged to have used cryptocurrency to pay an FBI undercover agent $3,000 as an advance.

name: Total payment for attacks
evidence_text: Another $7,000 was to follow if attacks on a synagogue and two Jewish community centres in the US had gone ahead.

name: arrests in Iran
evidence_text: HRANA said it had documented at least 4,023 arrests in Iran between 28 February and 9 May.

name: fatalities due to US-Israeli attacks
evidence_text: HRANA said it had documented at least 3,636 fatalities, including 1,701 civilians, due to US-Israeli attacks on Iran in the war.

name: executions of political prisoners
evidence_text: Rights groups have said that since the start of the war, Iran has executed 26 men seen as 'political prisoners.'
